title: The Second Resistance Against the Mongol-Yuan
summary: Tran Hung Dao with his 'Proclamation to the Officers' awakened patriotism, leading the army and people to defeat 500,000 enemy troops.

slides.text: This time, the Mongol-Yuan army brought 500,000 soldiers - a massive number! They aggressively demanded passage through our country to attack others.
slides.imagePrompt: A vast sea of Mongol soldiers and horses at the border, dust rising, overwhelming numbers, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Facing a strong enemy, Hung Dao Vuong Tran Hung Dao wrote the 'Proclamation to the Officers' (Hich Tuong Si) to boost morale. Soldiers tattooed 'Sat That' (Kill the Mongols) on their arms.
slides.imagePrompt: Soldiers around a campfire, showing their arms with 'SAT THAT' tattoos, look of determination and brotherhood, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: After many fierce battles, our troops won resounding victories at Ham Tu and Chuong Duong. The enemy panicked and scattered.
slides.imagePrompt: Victorious battle at a river port, enemy flags falling, Tran soldiers cheering, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Toghon - the enemy general - had to hide inside a copper tube for his soldiers to carry him away, escaping our pursuit. What a ridiculous sight for an invader!
slides.imagePrompt: Enemies carrying a large copper tube, a scared face looking out from one end, humorous cartoon style, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Why did the Tran Dynasty soldiers tattoo 'Sat That' on their arms?
options: To show their determination to kill the enemy and save the country
To recognize each other during battle
slides.answer: To show their determination to kill the enemy and save the country

slides.question: How did the enemy general Toghon escape?
